comment_text: The sad fact that gets overlooked here too is that when someone goes homeless, which many of those unable to pay rent already have, it takes even more money to get on your feet and stabilize.  Especially if a person has been evicted, many landlords take advantage of that and will charge a higher deposit to move into a place.  So, the $2,000 checks are not out of line.  Even for those who have not lost income during the pandemic, prices of common items have been driven up due to the panic buying.  Don't expect any of the talking heads in Washington to understand that.  They don't pay attention to that sort of thing because when you can always afford things instead of running a tight household budget, the increase in cost doesn't phase you.  It's going to be a long and painful road back, if we make it back!

In fact, I wonder how it is possible to compare the adverse reactions of those who have taken a vaccine with those who have not taken it, since the only element in common that binds the two groups is only a skin injection and not what was injected in the body? Apart from the material consequences of this injection, in fact, the adverse reactions of the control group were caused by the intake of the vaccine, but those of the placebo group from what? comment_text: The statement that implies that it "works best" if you get the second dose at three weeks is unproven, and probably not true. It just hasn't been tested. The clinical trials focused on the one scenario - getting the second dose at three weeks - and therefore the data from that trial is only really valid if that same protocol is followed. We may learn quickly that a delayed second dose may actually even prove better. I'm guessing that the reason that they didn't test other timeframes was because of the extreme urgency to get a vaccine out. Given that covid-19 will probably continue to be in human reservoirs for a long time (esp considering we haven't managed to totally eradicate polio), they'll probably end up finding the best way in coming months and years.

title: My COVID VACCINE Side Effects
comment_text: Multiple staff for our company reported pain at the injection site for a few days which is expected for intramuscular injections and was no surprise. No other symptoms reported from the 8 or so colleagues I trust and communicate daily with. Second round of the Pfizer vaccine was significantly worse. 6 of the 8 have received their second dose and besides the injection site pain, they all had varying temperature fevers 100-102.6,  severe fatigue , body aches to varying degrees, chills, and overall flu like symptoms. Some also complained of skin sensitivity and nausea. Symptom onset for the 6 staff who have taken the second dose all began approx 24-30 hours after it was administered and symptoms are lasting from 1-2 days. Some are still out after leaving work early the day after administration (most received the vaccine just before 8am) so it is unclear how long some of them will continue to experience symptoms. Age ranges and health conditions vary but all are between 35 and 60.

comment_text: I see that there are many who, although not in favor of the vaccine by conviction, are nevertheless willing to accept it in the hope that, once vaccinated, everything will go back to the way it was before. For the benefit of all, but especially of these people, I would like to highlight a passage from a conversation, which I translated, which recently took place between some scientists and the FDA expert on the efficacy of the vaccine and the evolution of the virus, which can be read in original on the official website of FDA.  "DR. ERIC TOPOL: I'm really glad you mentioned it, John, because I wanted to ask Steve and Peter. Because we have this so called B117 variant in the UK and other countries, but mostly in the UK. It has 23 mutations. There are a couple of them in spike protein that are particularly incriminating. And it is also one of those mutations shared by a South African variant, this so-called N501Y But what I wanted to ask Steve and Peter was, OK, we'll probably see further drift and evolution of this virus. We are already starting to see it beyond the pandemic dominant 614, the D614G. So at some point there will be a need for booster shots, whether it's a year or every time ”.  No wonder, given the AIFA statements some time ago, contained in faq no. 31:  "Faq n. 31 - Question: Does vaccination allow you to go back to life as before? Answer: Even if the effectiveness of the COVID-19 mRNA BNT162b2 vaccine is very high (over 90%) there will always be a portion of vaccinated who will not develop the immune defense, moreover, we still do not know definitively if vaccination only prevents the manifestation of the disease or even the transmission of the infection. This is why being vaccinated does not confer a "certificate of freedom" but it is necessary to continue to adopt correct behaviors and measures to contain the risk of infection "  In my opinion, apart from the considerable risks of a vaccine with many unknowns, our freedom will remain even more constrained than it is already now, having to submit to a continuous and constant mass vaccination whose consequences are currently unfathomable. .

title: Covaxin Safest Covid Vaccine In The World: Krishna Ella, Bharat Biotech CMD Reacts To Vaccine Row
comment_text: Can politicians opposing Covaxin explain the death of a nurse with no prior preconditions in Portugal after receiving the Pfizer vaccine? Let this idiocy stop. On advise of Indian scientists and scientists of Indian origin from abroad, the government has allowed Covaxin to collect more data of efficacy in vaccination program in a trial mode. To further clarify, only the vaccines from Astra Zeneca (Covishield), Pfizer and Moderna have efficacy data, but that was conducted on a small cohort size of a few thousand people and means nothing. While Pfizer and Moderna use the novel mRNA technology, Astra Zeneca uses Chimpanzee Adenovirus for gene delivery. On the other hand, Covaxin uses a much more proven technique (akin to pulse polio) and immune system gets the opportunity to recognize a much bigger array of SARS-CoV2 epitopes. This theoretically should give a wider range of protection, especially given the fact that multiple new strains are emerging. All the 4 vaccines have shown various levels (and encouraging levels) of immune response and people must get vaccinated. Humanity has suffered a lot. These are all first generation vaccines, and their real efficacies, counter indications and how long can they give protection can only be judged only after tens of thousand people are vaccinated and response is monitored over a period of 1 year. This is a learning process for scientists and doctors. Given the aggressiveness of the disease, if we go on the path of how MERS vaccine is being tested, we would have lost millions of lives. Since safety has been established in trials, it is important that people get vaccinated to stop the pandemic or at least slow it down. Yes, despite trials on safety, there can be counter-indications as happened with Pfizer vaccine in one case so far. Scientists will investigate and learn from it. But for some exceptional cases, the whole humanity can not be put under risk. Even scientists are afraid that the vaccines of Pfizer, Moderna and Astra Zeneca may not be as effective against mutant strains. Covaxin might actually give the cover there as it uses a very wide array of epitopes. I hope this helps. Also I would request all news agencies, please don't invite idiots in your show to give expert comments. They are just idiots pretending as experts. This wide world has many renowned scientists to guide people on this serious issue.
